description: A complete AI-powered development intelligence suite for Java/Angular mono-repos. Covers the full SDLC: architecture analysis, requirements documentation, persona-based review, test automation, and production risk detection.
author: Generated by Claude

skills.description: Scan a mono-repo and build a full hierarchical dependency graph. Emits repo-graph.json (machine-readable) and repo-graph.html (interactive D3.js visualiser). Must be run first — all other skills consume its output.

Three agents that consume repo-graph.json without re-scanning code. (1) GRAPH-QA: answer any natural-language question about the codebase. (2) FRD: generate functional requirements using graph as a navigation map. (3) API-PERF: identify API performance risks from graph structure.

agents.name: FRD
agents.purpose: FRD generation guided by graph structure

skills.description: Generate a complete 10-section Functional Requirements Document from Java/Angular code. Graph-accelerated: uses repo-graph.json when available to skip directory scanning. Written entirely in business language — no technical jargon. Output is a professional Word .docx.
Five expert personas that review the same material through different lenses. Can be triggered individually or as a round-table. Each persona has a distinct voice, output format, and set of concerns.

personas.name: Alex (Product Owner)
personas.signatureQuestion: Why does a user care about this?

personas.name: Jordan (Business Analyst)
personas.signatureQuestion: Is this complete, unambiguous, and testable?

personas.name: Morgan (Technical Architect)
personas.signatureQuestion: What happens under stress or change?

personas.name: Sam (Developer)
personas.signatureQuestion: How would I build this, and what will break?

personas.name: Riley (Test Architect)
personas.signatureQuestion: How do we prove this works?

skills.description: Generate complete, runnable Playwright test suites from real codebase analysis. Two suites: (1) Angular UI tests using Page Object Model, (2) Java API tests using APIRequestContext. Shared auth helpers, test data factories, and environment config.

Four-engine production risk detector. Finds what is missing, broken, or dangerous before production does. Engines: (1) Functional gaps — incomplete user journeys. (2) Technical gaps — broken contracts, missing error handling. (3) Scenario gaps — edge cases that break production. (4) Performance gaps — N+1, missing pagination, chatty services.

engines.name: Functional Gap Detector
engines.finds: Incomplete user journeys, missing CRUD, undocumented features

engines.name: Technical Gap Detector
engines.finds: Circular deps, missing error handling, dead code in requirements

engines.name: Scenario Gap Detector
engines.finds: Edge cases that break production: concurrency, boundaries, session, data integrity

engines.name: Performance Gap Detector
engines.finds: N+1 queries, missing pagination, chatty services, blocking calls

recommendedWorkflows.name: Full Discovery
recommendedWorkflows.description: Complete first-time analysis of a new codebase

recommendedWorkflows.name: Quick Module Analysis
recommendedWorkflows.description: Rapid analysis of a single module

recommendedWorkflows.name: Pre-Release Risk Review
recommendedWorkflows.description: Risk check before a production release

recommendedWorkflows.name: Test Suite Generation
recommendedWorkflows.description: Build automated tests for a feature or module
